description: Display personal information section including full name, professional title, profile photo, contact details (email, phone, location, LinkedIn, GitHub, portfolio links). This is the header/hero section that creates the first impression for visitors.
steps: Navigate to the CV website homepage
Verify personal name and professional title are prominently displayed
Verify profile photo is visible and properly sized
Verify all contact information and social links are displayed and clickable
Verify layout is visually appealing and professional

description: Display a professional summary or about me section that provides a brief overview of the person's background, expertise, and career objectives. This helps visitors quickly understand the candidate's value proposition.
steps: Navigate to the CV website
Locate the professional summary section
Verify summary text is displayed clearly and is readable
Verify section has appropriate heading/title
Verify text formatting (paragraphs, line breaks) renders correctly

description: Display work experience section showing job history with company names, job titles, employment dates, locations, and detailed descriptions of responsibilities and achievements. This is critical for showcasing professional background.
steps: Navigate to the work experience section
Verify all job entries are displayed in reverse chronological order
Verify each entry shows company name, job title, dates, and location
Verify job descriptions and achievements are displayed
Verify visual hierarchy makes information easy to scan

description: Display education section showing academic qualifications including institution names, degrees/certifications, fields of study, graduation dates, and relevant achievements (GPA, honors, relevant coursework).
steps: Navigate to the education section
Verify all educational entries are displayed
Verify each entry shows institution, degree, field of study, and dates
Verify additional details like honors or GPA are displayed if present
Verify entries are in reverse chronological order

description: Display skills section showcasing technical and soft skills, potentially organized by category (programming languages, frameworks, tools, soft skills). May include proficiency levels or visual indicators.
steps: Navigate to the skills section
Verify all skills are displayed and organized logically
Verify skill categories or groupings are clear
Verify proficiency levels or indicators are shown if applicable
Verify skills are easy to scan and visually appealing
